description_long: This object is a pair of vintage-style over-ear headphones with a simple, utilitarian design. The headphones feature two round ear cups connected by a thin, padded headband, with visible hinges and adjustment points on either side. A long, coiled cable extends from one ear cup, ending in a standard audio jack plug, suggesting compatibility with a variety of audio devices. The ear cups and headband appear to be constructed from black plastic and metal, with some cushioning visible on the headband for comfort. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the cable is loosely coiled, indicating flexibility. This type of object would typically be found in urban environments such as recording studios, radio stations, or used by individuals in public spaces for personal audio listening.
description: A pair of vintage-style over-ear headphones with round black ear cups, a padded headband, and a long coiled audio cable ending in a standard jack.
description_view_0: Front view showing both round ear cups, the padded headband, and the cable extending forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the ear cups, the curvature of the headband, and the cable trailing to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the ear cups, the headband arch, and the cable with its plug visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the ear cups stacked, the headband curve, and the cable extending outward.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
